rationale: This occupation scores high on disruption (8) because the core work involves digital data analysis, database management, coding classification systems, and generating reports - all tasks that AI excels at and is rapidly improving in. AI can already automate much of the data abstraction, validation, coding, and report generation that comprises the majority of daily tasks. The elasticity score is low (3) because demand for health information management is largely fixed by regulatory requirements, existing patient populations, and healthcare system capacity - making healthcare data 10x cheaper to process won't create 10x more patients or fundamentally expand the market. This combination of high disruption and low elasticity places it in the 'replace' category, where AI productivity gains lead to reduced headcount rather than expanded demand.

title: Heating, air conditioning, and refrigeration mechanics and installers
rationale: HVAC work is predominantly physical and hands-on, involving installation, repair, and maintenance of complex mechanical systems in real-world environments that require human presence, dexterity, and problem-solving in unpredictable conditions. While AI might assist with some diagnostic tools, scheduling, or record-keeping, the core tasks of physically installing equipment, crawling through tight spaces, handling refrigerants, and troubleshooting mechanical failures remain largely outside AI's capabilities. Demand elasticity is low-to-moderate because HVAC needs are primarily driven by building construction, equipment replacement cycles, and regulatory requirements rather than cost sensitivity - making systems cheaper wouldn't dramatically increase the number of buildings needing climate control. The occupation remains resilient as AI cannot meaningfully reshape the fundamentally physical and situational nature of the work.

title: Heavy and tractor-trailer truck drivers
rationale: While autonomous vehicle technology poses a significant long-term threat to truck driving, current AI cannot handle the complex real-world scenarios, regulatory requirements, cargo handling, and safety inspections that define this occupation. The work remains predominantly physical and requires human presence for unpredictable situations. Demand elasticity is low because freight transportation needs are relatively fixed - making trucking cheaper wouldn't dramatically increase the total volume of goods that need to be moved across the economy. The disruption score of 6 places this at the boundary, but given current technological limitations and regulatory barriers, the occupation remains resilient in the near-to-medium term.

title: Historians
rationale: Historians' work involves substantial knowledge work - research, analysis, interpretation, and writing - which AI can assist with through document analysis, pattern recognition, and writing support, earning a disruption score of 6. However, the core work requires deep contextual understanding, critical thinking about authenticity and significance, and human judgment that AI cannot fully replicate. Demand elasticity is low (4) because historical research is primarily driven by institutional needs, academic requirements, and preservation mandates rather than cost considerations - making history cheaper doesn't create substantially more demand for historical analysis. With moderate disruption but the score falling at the boundary, this occupation is resilient because AI enhances rather than transforms the fundamental nature of historical scholarship.

title: Home health and personal care aides
rationale: This occupation scores low on disruption because the core work is predominantly physical, hands-on care requiring human presence, empathy, and real-world navigation - AI can only assist with peripheral tasks like scheduling or basic monitoring. Elasticity is high due to massive unmet demand driven by an aging population and preference shifts toward home-based care over institutional settings, meaning productivity gains would unlock significant latent demand for personalized care services. However, with disruption at only 3, this falls into the resilient category where AI touches the periphery but doesn't reshape the fundamental nature of providing physical assistance, companionship, and human care.

title: Human resources managers
rationale: HR management involves substantial knowledge work that AI can increasingly handle - recruiting/screening candidates, policy development, compliance monitoring, data analysis, and strategic planning are all areas where AI tools are already making significant inroads. However, demand for HR services is relatively inelastic as organizations have fairly fixed needs for workforce management, regulatory compliance, and employee relations regardless of cost. While some efficiency gains might allow for slightly more strategic HR work, the core demand is structurally limited by organizational size and regulatory requirements, making this a classic case where productivity gains lead to workforce reduction rather than expanded demand.

title: Hydrologists
rationale: Hydrologists face moderate disruption as AI can enhance data analysis, modeling, and report generation, but core work requires field sampling, equipment operation, and physical presence in diverse outdoor environments. Demand elasticity is low because water management needs are largely driven by regulatory requirements, infrastructure projects, and environmental compliance rather than cost considerations - making water 10x cheaper to analyze doesn't create 10x more rivers to study. The disruption score of 6 places this in the resilient category, where AI augments analytical capabilities but doesn't fundamentally reshape the occupation's field-based, scientific methodology.

title: Industrial designers
rationale: Industrial design is highly disrupted by AI as core tasks like sketching, 3D modeling, prototyping, and design iteration are increasingly AI-assisted through generative design tools, automated CAD workflows, and AI-powered visualization. The work is predominantly digital and cognitive, making it directly in AI's path. Demand elasticity is high because cheaper and faster design capabilities will unlock significant latent demand - more products can be designed and iterated, smaller companies can afford professional design services, and the barrier to bringing new products to market drops substantially. While AI transforms how designers work, the expanded market for designed products should absorb productivity gains and maintain or grow employment.

title: Industrial engineers
rationale: Industrial engineers' core work involves data analysis, process optimization, system design, and efficiency modeling - predominantly knowledge work that AI tools can significantly enhance through automated data collection, predictive analytics, and optimization algorithms. However, the role requires substantial human judgment for stakeholder collaboration, implementation oversight, and contextual decision-making in complex manufacturing and service environments. Demand elasticity is moderate because while AI-enhanced efficiency analysis could unlock optimization opportunities in more organizations and enable deeper analysis of existing systems, the total number of facilities and processes requiring industrial engineering is somewhat constrained by the physical economy's growth rate.

title: Industrial machinery mechanics, machinery maintenance workers, and millwrights
rationale: This occupation scores low on disruption because the core work is predominantly physical and hands-on - disassembling machinery, welding, using hand tools, moving heavy equipment, and performing repairs that require direct manipulation of industrial systems. While AI may assist with some diagnostic tasks and technical manual interpretation, the fundamental work of physically maintaining, repairing, and installing industrial machinery cannot be automated. Elasticity is low-moderate because demand for machinery maintenance is largely tied to existing industrial capacity and regulatory requirements rather than cost sensitivity - factories need a baseline level of maintenance regardless of price, though some efficiency gains might enable slightly more preventive maintenance. The resilient category fits because AI touches only peripheral aspects like diagnostics while leaving the core physical work unchanged.

title: Information security analysts
rationale: Information security work is highly digital and cognitive, involving threat analysis, system monitoring, and security protocol development - areas where AI tools are already providing significant assistance in threat detection, vulnerability assessment, and automated response systems. However, the core work still requires human judgment for complex threat analysis, strategic security planning, and organizational communication. Demand elasticity is high because cybersecurity needs are currently severely undersupplied - most organizations lack adequate security coverage, and as AI makes security analysis faster and cheaper, it enables broader protection across more systems, smaller organizations, and emerging technologies, creating substantial latent demand that will absorb productivity gains.

title: Instructional coordinators
rationale: Instructional coordinators face high disruption as their core tasks—curriculum development, data analysis, training design, and educational material creation—are predominantly knowledge-based work that AI can significantly enhance or automate. AI tools can already analyze student performance data, generate curriculum content, create training materials, and provide personalized learning recommendations. However, demand elasticity is moderate because while cheaper curriculum development and data analysis could enable more schools to access quality instructional coordination, growth is constrained by fixed institutional structures, budget limitations, and regulatory requirements in education systems. The combination of high disruption with moderate elasticity suggests the role will survive but require substantial skill adaptation toward AI tool management, human relationship building, and strategic oversight.

title: Insulation workers
rationale: Insulation work is almost entirely physical and hands-on, requiring workers to measure, cut, fit, and install materials in confined spaces, work at heights, and handle specialized tools in real-world construction environments. AI has minimal relevance to the core tasks of physically manipulating insulation materials, working in cramped spaces, and ensuring proper installation around pipes and structures. While demand for insulation may increase somewhat due to energy efficiency trends, it's largely constrained by the fixed number of buildings being constructed or renovated, limiting elasticity. The occupation remains resilient as the core work cannot be digitized or automated with current or near-term AI capabilities.

title: Interior designers
rationale: Interior design involves substantial digital work including CAD software, 3D visualization, material specification, and project planning that AI can significantly enhance or automate, earning a disruption score of 7. However, the work still requires client consultation, site visits, aesthetic judgment, and coordination with contractors. Demand elasticity is moderate (6) because while cheaper design services could unlock some latent demand from smaller businesses and homeowners currently priced out, the market is constrained by the physical reality of construction projects, regulatory requirements, and the fact that people don't redesign spaces frequently regardless of cost. The combination of high disruption and moderate elasticity places this in the restructure category, where designers who adapt to AI tools will thrive while those who don't may be displaced.

title: Interpreters and translators
rationale: AI translation and interpretation tools have already significantly disrupted this field, with machine translation handling basic text conversion and real-time interpretation becoming increasingly sophisticated. The core work is entirely digital/linguistic and directly in AI's path, earning a high disruption score. However, demand elasticity is moderate - while cheaper translation services could expand access to multilingual content and communication, growth is constrained by the finite number of cross-language interactions needed in specific contexts like courts, hospitals, and conferences. The combination of high disruption and moderate elasticity suggests the occupation will restructure, with workers needing to specialize in complex, culturally-sensitive, or real-time interactive scenarios where human expertise remains essential.

title: Kindergarten and elementary school teachers
rationale: AI significantly disrupts many core teaching tasks including lesson planning, content creation, grading, assessment, and personalized learning support, but the essential human elements of classroom management, emotional support, and real-time student interaction remain critical. Demand elasticity is high because AI-powered educational tools can dramatically reduce the cost of personalized instruction, potentially unlocking massive latent demand for individualized tutoring, adaptive learning programs, and educational support that was previously unaffordable at scale. While AI will transform how teachers work by automating administrative tasks and enabling more personalized instruction, the expanded access to quality education will likely maintain or increase overall demand for teaching professionals.

title: Librarians and library media specialists
rationale: Librarians face moderate disruption as AI can assist with information retrieval, cataloging, and research guidance, but the core work remains heavily interpersonal - teaching patrons, program planning, community engagement, and providing personalized research assistance require human judgment and social skills. Demand elasticity is low because library usage is largely driven by institutional needs (schools, public services) and community programming rather than cost considerations - making libraries 10x more efficient wouldn't dramatically increase patron visits or expand library services beyond current capacity constraints. The disruption score of 6 places this in the resilient category, where AI enhances but doesn't fundamentally reshape the occupation's core human-centered responsibilities.

title: Loan officers
rationale: Loan officers face high disruption as their core tasks—analyzing financial data, credit assessment, underwriting, and loan processing—are predominantly digital and analytical work that AI can increasingly automate. Modern underwriting software already handles much of the decision-making process, and AI can enhance customer service interactions, document processing, and risk assessment. However, demand elasticity is low because loan demand is primarily driven by economic conditions, interest rates, and regulatory requirements rather than the cost or speed of loan processing. Making loans faster or cheaper doesn't significantly increase the number of people or businesses seeking credit, as borrowing is constrained by creditworthiness and economic need rather than processing efficiency.

title: Logging workers
rationale: Logging work is almost entirely physical and hands-on, involving operating heavy machinery in unpredictable outdoor environments, cutting trees with chainsaws, and handling logs in rough terrain - tasks that AI cannot perform and that require human presence, strength, and real-time decision-making in hazardous conditions. While some peripheral tasks like log grading data entry might benefit from AI tools, the core work remains fundamentally unchanged. Demand for logging services is relatively inelastic, constrained by forest management needs, construction demand, and environmental regulations rather than cost, so productivity gains would not significantly expand total demand for logging workers.

title: Manicurists and pedicurists
rationale: Manicurists and pedicurists perform predominantly hands-on, physical work requiring manual dexterity, direct client interaction, and precise manipulation of tools on human nails and skin - tasks that AI cannot perform. While AI might assist with peripheral business functions like scheduling or inventory management, the core work of cleaning, shaping, polishing nails and providing personal care services remains entirely human-dependent. Demand elasticity is low-to-moderate because nail services are already relatively affordable luxury services, and while some expansion is possible through mobile services or new demographics, the market is not heavily constrained by cost barriers that AI productivity gains could meaningfully address. The low disruption score places this occupation firmly in the resilient category.

title: Marine engineers and naval architects
rationale: Marine engineering and naval architecture involves substantial digital design work including CAD, system layouts, detailed drawings, performance modeling, and technical documentation - all areas where AI tools are already proving valuable for design optimization, simulation, and documentation. However, the work requires deep understanding of complex physical systems, regulatory compliance, safety standards, and often involves hands-on testing and field work. Demand elasticity is moderate because while AI could make ship design faster and cheaper, the market is constrained by the limited number of shipbuilding projects, regulatory approval processes, and the fact that most vessels are large capital investments with long lifecycles rather than mass-market products.

title: Market research analysts
rationale: Market research analysts face high disruption as their core tasks—data collection, statistical analysis, trend forecasting, and report generation—are predominantly digital and cognitive work that AI tools can already meaningfully assist with or automate. AI excels at processing large datasets, identifying patterns, generating insights, and creating visualizations. However, demand elasticity is high because cheaper and faster market research unlocks significant latent demand—more businesses can afford comprehensive market analysis, more products can be tested, and more granular consumer insights become accessible to smaller companies that previously couldn't afford such services. The net effect is augmentation rather than replacement because the expanded market for research services absorbs the productivity gains.

title: Marriage and family therapists
rationale: Marriage and family therapy involves deep human connection, emotional intelligence, and real-time interpersonal dynamics that AI cannot replicate, though AI may assist with administrative tasks, treatment planning, and resource recommendations. The demand for mental health services is highly elastic - there is massive unmet need due to cost and access barriers, and cheaper/more accessible therapy would dramatically expand utilization across all demographics. However, since the core therapeutic relationship and emotional processing work remains fundamentally human-centered with only moderate AI disruption, this occupation falls into the resilient category despite high demand elasticity.

title: Masonry workers
rationale: Masonry work is almost entirely physical and hands-on, involving lifting heavy materials, precise manual placement of bricks/stones, and working with mortar in unpredictable outdoor environments. AI has minimal relevance to the core tasks of cutting, laying, and finishing masonry materials. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like reading blueprints or calculating materials, the fundamental work requires human dexterity, physical strength, and real-world problem-solving. Demand elasticity is low because construction needs are largely driven by population growth, infrastructure requirements, and building codes rather than cost - making masonry 10x cheaper wouldn't dramatically increase the number of buildings constructed.

title: Material moving machine operators
rationale: Material moving machine operators work in predominantly physical environments requiring real-world spatial awareness, manual dexterity, and safety coordination that AI cannot replicate. While some peripheral tasks like record-keeping and route optimization may benefit from AI assistance, the core work of operating heavy machinery, navigating complex physical spaces, and ensuring worker safety remains fundamentally human-dependent. Demand for material moving is relatively inelastic as it's tied to fixed physical infrastructure needs - warehouses don't suddenly need 10x more forklifts if AI makes operations more efficient, and construction sites have structural limits on material flow regardless of technological improvements. The moderate disruption score reflects AI's limited impact on core physical operations, making this occupation resilient to AI displacement.

title: Mathematicians and statisticians
rationale: Mathematicians and statisticians face very high disruption as their core work—data analysis, statistical modeling, pattern recognition, and mathematical computation—is directly in AI's wheelhouse, with AI tools already transforming how statistical analysis and mathematical modeling are performed. However, demand elasticity is high because cheaper and faster mathematical analysis unlocks significant latent demand across industries; businesses that previously couldn't afford sophisticated statistical analysis can now access it, expanding the total market for mathematical insights. The combination of high disruption with high elasticity suggests workers will produce dramatically more output per person, with overall employment likely stable or growing as the expanded market absorbs productivity gains.

title: Medical assistants
rationale: Medical assistants perform a mix of administrative tasks (scheduling, data entry, billing) that AI can automate and clinical tasks (taking vital signs, patient interaction, specimen collection) that require physical presence and human touch. While AI can streamline documentation and administrative workflows, the core clinical duties involving direct patient care remain largely unchanged. Demand elasticity is moderate because cheaper administrative processing could enable more efficient healthcare delivery and potentially expand access to care, but growth is constrained by the need for human clinical interaction and regulatory requirements. The disruption score of 5 places this in the resilient category, as AI enhances rather than transforms the fundamental nature of the work.

title: Medical equipment repairers
rationale: Medical equipment repair is predominantly hands-on physical work requiring on-site presence, manual dexterity, and real-world troubleshooting of complex electromechanical systems. While AI may assist with diagnostic software and documentation, the core tasks of disassembling equipment, replacing parts, calibrating machines, and performing preventive maintenance remain fundamentally manual. Demand elasticity is low because medical facilities have fixed equipment needs based on patient capacity and regulatory requirements - making repairs 10x faster wouldn't lead hospitals to purchase dramatically more equipment or need proportionally more repair services. The moderate disruption score and low elasticity place this firmly in the resilient category.

title: Medical transcriptionists
rationale: Medical transcription is almost entirely digital work involving converting speech to text, which AI speech recognition and natural language processing can now do with high accuracy. The BLS already notes that speech recognition technology is reducing demand for transcriptionists and increasing productivity of remaining workers. Demand for medical transcription services is highly inelastic - healthcare providers need a fixed amount of documentation regardless of cost, and patients don't generate more medical encounters just because transcription becomes cheaper. The combination of very high AI disruption and very low demand elasticity makes this a clear replacement scenario.

title: Meeting, convention, and event planners
rationale: Event planning involves significant knowledge work that AI can assist with - vendor research, scheduling optimization, budget tracking, communication drafting, and logistics coordination - but requires substantial human presence for client relationships, venue inspections, real-time problem solving, and on-site event management. The demand elasticity is high because cheaper, more efficient event planning could unlock significant latent demand from smaller businesses, individuals, and organizations that currently forgo professional planning due to cost, while existing clients might host more frequent or elaborate events. The combination of meaningful AI assistance with strong demand expansion suggests workers will become more productive rather than displaced.

title: Microbiologists
rationale: Microbiologists face moderate-to-high disruption as AI increasingly assists with data analysis, pattern recognition in microscopic images, genetic sequencing analysis, and research literature review, but core laboratory work like specimen collection, culture maintenance, and experimental design still requires human expertise and physical presence. Demand elasticity is high because cheaper and faster microbial analysis could dramatically expand applications in personalized medicine, environmental monitoring, food safety, and biotechnology development - areas currently limited by cost and throughput constraints. The combination of meaningful AI assistance with strong demand expansion potential places this in the augment category, where productivity gains drive market growth rather than job displacement.

title: Middle school teachers
rationale: Middle school teaching involves substantial knowledge work - lesson planning, assessment, content delivery, and individualized instruction - that AI can meaningfully assist with through automated grading, personalized learning platforms, and curriculum generation. However, the core human elements of classroom management, emotional support, and real-time adaptation to student needs remain essential. Demand elasticity is high because cheaper, AI-enhanced education could unlock massive latent demand for personalized tutoring, smaller class sizes, and more individualized attention that is currently constrained by cost and teacher availability. The combination of moderate-to-high disruption with high elasticity suggests teachers will become more productive rather than replaced.

title: Mining and geological engineers
rationale: Mining and geological engineers face moderate disruption as AI can assist with data analysis, mine design optimization, and report generation, but core work requires physical site evaluation, safety oversight, and complex engineering judgment in unpredictable real-world environments. Demand elasticity is low because mining operations are driven by commodity needs and regulatory requirements rather than cost - making mining 10x cheaper wouldn't create 10x more demand for minerals, and mine locations are geologically constrained. The disruption score of 6 places this in the resilient category, where AI enhances but doesn't fundamentally reshape the core engineering and safety oversight responsibilities.

title: Music directors and composers
rationale: AI significantly disrupts composition work through tools that can generate melodies, harmonies, and arrangements, and assists with scoring and orchestration, but music direction requires real-time human leadership of live performers that AI cannot replicate. Demand elasticity is high because AI-assisted composition dramatically lowers barriers to music creation, enabling more content for films, games, commercials, and independent artists who previously couldn't afford custom compositions. While AI transforms the compositional toolkit, the expanded market for affordable, customized music should create more opportunities overall for music professionals who adapt to AI-augmented workflows.

title: Natural sciences managers
rationale: Natural sciences managers have moderate disruption as AI can assist with data analysis, report generation, and project monitoring, but the core work requires human judgment for strategic planning, team leadership, stakeholder communication, and complex decision-making in research environments. Elasticity is low because demand for management positions is relatively fixed - organizations need a certain number of managers regardless of AI productivity gains, and regulatory/structural constraints limit expansion of management roles. The disruption score of 6 places this occupation in the resilient category, where AI enhances peripheral tasks but doesn't fundamentally reshape the core managerial and leadership responsibilities.

title: News analysts, reporters, and journalists
rationale: AI significantly disrupts journalism's core tasks - research, writing, fact-checking, and content creation are all digital knowledge work that AI tools already assist with meaningfully, and the trajectory points toward AI handling much more of the information gathering, analysis, and writing process. However, demand elasticity is low because news consumption is relatively fixed - people don't consume dramatically more news just because it's cheaper to produce, and advertising revenue (the primary funding source) is already declining regardless of production costs. The combination of high disruption to core digital tasks and constrained demand growth due to structural industry challenges points to significant headcount contraction.

title: Nurse anesthetists, nurse midwives, and nurse practitioners
rationale: APRNs perform highly complex clinical work requiring physical examination, patient interaction, critical decision-making in unpredictable situations, and hands-on procedures like administering anesthesia or delivering babies. While AI can assist with diagnostic support, treatment planning, and documentation, the core work remains fundamentally human-centered and requires physical presence, clinical judgment, and interpersonal skills that AI cannot replicate. Demand elasticity is high due to aging populations, chronic disease growth, and healthcare access gaps that cheaper/faster care could address, but the moderate disruption score keeps this in the resilient category. The 35% projected job growth reflects strong underlying demand that isn't primarily driven by AI productivity gains.

title: Operations research analysts
rationale: Operations research analysts work almost entirely with digital data, mathematical modeling, and quantitative analysis - all core AI capabilities. AI tools can already automate much of the data collection, statistical analysis, and model development that forms their daily work, earning a high disruption score. However, demand for optimization and data-driven decision making is currently constrained by cost and availability of skilled analysts. As AI makes these services dramatically cheaper and faster, organizations will likely expand their use of operations research across more business units, processes, and decisions that previously couldn't justify the expense. The net effect is augmentation as productivity gains unlock significant latent demand for analytical insights.

title: Opticians
rationale: Opticians' work involves significant hands-on physical tasks like measuring faces, adjusting frames, fitting contact lenses, and providing personalized customer service that requires human presence and dexterity. While AI could assist with some administrative tasks like inventory management or basic prescription interpretation, the core work of physical fitting, adjustment, and face-to-face customer consultation remains largely unchanged. Demand elasticity is low because eyewear needs are primarily driven by medical necessity rather than cost - people don't suddenly need 10x more glasses if they become cheaper, and the market is already well-served. The moderate disruption score combined with structural demand constraints places this firmly in the resilient category.

title: Optometrists
rationale: Optometry involves significant diagnostic and analytical work that AI can enhance through automated vision testing, image analysis of retinal scans, and pattern recognition for eye diseases, but the profession requires extensive patient interaction, physical examination skills, and clinical judgment that keep humans central to care delivery. Demand elasticity is high because vision care is currently limited by cost and access - cheaper, faster diagnostic tools could enable more frequent screenings, expand care to underserved populations, and catch problems earlier when treatment is more effective. The combination of meaningful AI disruption to diagnostic tasks with strong demand expansion potential positions optometry for augmentation rather than replacement.

title: Orthotists and prosthetists
rationale: Disruption is moderate because while AI could assist with design optimization, material selection, and documentation, the core work requires extensive hands-on patient interaction, physical measurements, custom fitting, and manual fabrication skills that demand real-world presence and tactile expertise. Elasticity is moderate-to-high as cheaper/faster design and manufacturing could expand access to prosthetics and orthotics for underserved populations and enable more frequent device updates, but growth is constrained by the specialized medical nature and insurance/regulatory frameworks. Since disruption remains below the high threshold, this occupation is resilient to AI transformation.

title: Painting and coating workers
rationale: This occupation involves predominantly physical, hands-on work including surface preparation, manual application of coatings, equipment operation, and quality inspection that requires real-world presence and tactile skills. While some administrative tasks and automated systems may benefit from AI assistance, the core work of physically applying paints and coatings to products remains largely unchanged by current AI capabilities. Demand for painting and coating services is relatively inelastic - tied to fixed manufacturing volumes and repair needs rather than cost sensitivity, so productivity gains would not significantly expand total market demand. The combination of low disruption and inelastic demand places this firmly in the resilient category.

title: Pest control workers
rationale: Pest control work is fundamentally physical and hands-on, requiring workers to physically inspect buildings, crawl in tight spaces, apply treatments, and handle equipment in real-world environments that AI cannot navigate. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like scheduling or basic pest identification, the core work of physically accessing infested areas, applying treatments, and ensuring safety remains entirely manual. Demand for pest control is relatively inelastic - people need pest problems solved when they occur, but cheaper service wouldn't dramatically increase the frequency of infestations or create new demand beyond current needs. The low disruption score keeps this occupation in the resilient category despite low elasticity.

title: Petroleum engineers
rationale: Petroleum engineering involves substantial analytical and design work that AI can assist with (data analysis, reservoir modeling, optimization calculations), but the core work requires deep domain expertise, physical site presence, and integration with complex real-world drilling operations. While AI tools are becoming useful for data analysis and simulation, the fundamental engineering judgment, safety oversight, and field operations management remain human-centric. Demand elasticity is very low because oil and gas extraction is driven by global energy needs and existing reserves, not by the cost of engineering services - making extraction 10x cheaper through AI wouldn't significantly increase the number of wells drilled or reservoirs developed. The net effect is resilient because moderate disruption combined with structural demand constraints means the occupation adapts to use AI tools without fundamental transformation.

title: Pharmacists
rationale: Pharmacists face moderate disruption as AI can assist with drug interaction checking, dosage calculations, and inventory management, but core responsibilities require physical medication dispensing, patient counseling, clinical judgment, and regulatory compliance that demand human oversight. Demand elasticity is low because prescription volumes are largely driven by population health needs and physician prescribing patterns rather than cost - making medications 10x cheaper to dispense wouldn't significantly increase the number of prescriptions people need. The combination of moderate disruption and low elasticity places this occupation in the resilient category, where AI enhances efficiency but doesn't fundamentally reshape the profession.

title: Phlebotomists
rationale: Phlebotomy is fundamentally a hands-on medical procedure requiring physical dexterity, patient interaction, and real-world presence - AI cannot draw blood or handle the sterile equipment and safety protocols involved. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like database entry or scheduling, the core work of venipuncture, patient care, and sample handling remains entirely manual. Demand elasticity is low because blood testing needs are medically determined rather than cost-driven - patients don't get more blood draws just because the process becomes more efficient. The occupation remains resilient as AI cannot meaningfully disrupt the essential physical and interpersonal aspects of the work.

title: Photographers
rationale: Photography faces high disruption as AI can now generate photorealistic images, automate post-processing, and enhance composition, fundamentally changing core creative and technical tasks. However, demand elasticity is moderate - while cheaper AI-generated imagery may expand some commercial uses, human photographers retain advantages in capturing authentic moments, working with live subjects, and providing personalized service that clients value. The occupation will survive but require photographers to adapt by specializing in areas where human presence and creativity remain essential, such as event photography, portraiture requiring interpersonal skills, and artistic work that emphasizes authentic human experience over pure image generation.

title: Physical therapist assistants and aides
rationale: Physical therapist assistants and aides perform predominantly hands-on, physical work requiring direct patient contact, manual therapy techniques, and real-world presence for safety and care quality - areas where AI has minimal impact. While AI might assist with some administrative tasks like scheduling or documentation, the core work of helping patients with exercises, providing manual therapy, observing patient responses, and ensuring safety during movement requires human touch and judgment. Demand for physical therapy services is highly elastic due to an aging population, chronic disease management needs, and currently unmet demand constrained by cost, suggesting that any productivity gains would likely expand access rather than reduce headcount. However, since AI disruption to the core work is minimal, this occupation remains resilient to AI transformation.

title: Physicians and surgeons
rationale: While AI is increasingly useful for diagnostic imaging analysis, clinical decision support, and administrative tasks, the core work of physicians requires complex human judgment, physical examination skills, patient interaction, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable medical situations that AI cannot fully replicate. The disruption is moderate because AI augments rather than replaces the fundamental physician-patient relationship and clinical reasoning. Demand elasticity is high because cheaper, faster medical services could dramatically expand access to healthcare, especially in underserved areas, and enable more preventive care and early interventions. However, since disruption remains moderate at 5, this falls into the resilient category where AI enhances but doesn't fundamentally reshape the core medical practice.

title: Preschool teachers
rationale: Preschool teaching involves substantial physical presence, emotional connection, and real-time adaptation to young children's needs - core activities that AI cannot replicate. While AI might assist with lesson planning, record-keeping, or creating educational materials, the fundamental work of nurturing, supervising, and teaching 3-5 year olds requires human interaction, physical care, and emotional intelligence. Demand elasticity is high because cheaper educational tools could make quality early childhood education more accessible, potentially expanding enrollment, but the disruption score of 4 keeps this in the resilient category since AI doesn't fundamentally change the core human-centered work.

title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
rationale: The disruption score is moderate because while AI can assist with report writing, case file management, and risk assessment tools, the core work requires extensive human interaction, relationship building, community supervision, and real-time judgment in unpredictable situations with high-risk individuals. The elasticity score is low because demand is largely determined by crime rates, court decisions, and government budgets rather than cost - making supervision 10x cheaper wouldn't lead to supervising 10x more offenders, as caseloads are driven by judicial and policy decisions. With moderate disruption and low elasticity, this occupation falls into the resilient category where AI enhances peripheral tasks but doesn't fundamentally reshape the human-centered core work.

title: Property appraisers and assessors
rationale: Property appraisal involves significant digital analysis (comparables research, data analysis, report writing) that AI can assist with, but requires physical property inspection, local market knowledge, and professional judgment for complex valuations, limiting disruption to moderate levels. Demand elasticity is low because property appraisals are typically required for specific transactions (sales, mortgages, tax assessments) with relatively fixed frequency - making properties 10x cheaper to appraise wouldn't dramatically increase the number of transactions requiring appraisals. The moderate disruption score keeps this in the resilient category, where AI enhances efficiency but doesn't fundamentally reshape the core work.

title: Psychologists
rationale: Psychology involves substantial cognitive work - assessment, diagnosis, research, and report writing - that AI can assist with, but the core therapeutic relationship, empathy, and complex human interaction remain fundamentally human. AI tools are already emerging for psychological assessment, treatment planning, and research analysis, placing this at moderate-high disruption. Demand elasticity is high because mental health services are severely undersupplied globally - if AI makes psychological services faster and more accessible, there's enormous latent demand from underserved populations who currently can't access care due to cost, availability, or stigma barriers. The combination of meaningful AI assistance with vast unmet demand suggests psychologists will be augmented rather than replaced.

title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
rationale: Purchasing work is highly digital and analytical - evaluating suppliers, analyzing price proposals, negotiating contracts, and monitoring performance metrics are all tasks where AI excels at data processing, pattern recognition, and optimization. AI tools can already automate supplier evaluation, contract analysis, and price comparison at scale. However, demand elasticity is low because organizations have relatively fixed procurement needs - making purchasing 10x faster doesn't mean companies suddenly need 10x more office supplies or raw materials. The efficiency gains will primarily translate to fewer purchasing professionals needed rather than expanded procurement activity.

title: Railroad workers
rationale: Railroad work is predominantly physical and requires real-world presence - operating trains, coupling cars, maintaining signals, and ensuring safety through direct observation and manual controls. While AI assists with some scheduling and monitoring systems, the core tasks of physically operating locomotives, managing freight, and ensuring safety through human judgment remain largely unchanged. Demand for rail transportation is relatively inelastic as it's driven by existing freight shipping needs and passenger routes rather than cost sensitivity - making trains 10x more efficient wouldn't dramatically increase the volume of goods needing transport or create new passenger demand. The moderate disruption score reflects some AI integration in logistics and monitoring, but the low elasticity and physical nature of the work make this occupation resilient to AI displacement.

title: Receptionists
rationale: Receptionist work is highly digital and cognitive - answering phones, scheduling appointments, data entry, and providing information are all tasks that AI chatbots, automated scheduling systems, and voice assistants can already handle effectively. Many organizations are already implementing these technologies to reduce reception staffing. Demand elasticity is very low because organizations need a fixed amount of front-desk coverage regardless of cost - making reception services cheaper doesn't create demand for more receptionists. The BLS already projects 0% growth, reflecting ongoing automation of these administrative functions.

title: Recreation workers
rationale: Recreation workers score low on disruption because their core work is fundamentally interpersonal and physical - leading activities, providing in-person instruction, ensuring safety, and motivating participants in real-world settings. While AI might help with some administrative tasks like scheduling or creating promotional materials, the essential human elements of group leadership, physical activity guidance, and social interaction cannot be meaningfully automated. Elasticity is moderate because cheaper recreational programming could expand access in underserved communities and enable more specialized activities, but demand is somewhat constrained by facility capacity, staffing ratios for safety, and the inherently local nature of most recreational services. With disruption at 3, this occupation falls clearly into the resilient category.

title: Recreational therapists
rationale: Recreational therapy requires substantial in-person interaction, physical presence with patients, hands-on activity facilitation, and real-time adaptation to patient needs and responses, limiting AI's ability to disrupt core functions. While AI could assist with treatment planning, progress documentation, and activity suggestions, the therapeutic relationship and physical engagement remain fundamentally human-centered. Demand shows moderate elasticity as cheaper/more efficient therapy could expand access in underserved populations and enable more preventive mental health interventions, but regulatory constraints and the inherently personal nature of therapy limit dramatic expansion. The disruption score of 4 places this firmly in the resilient category, as AI touches peripheral administrative tasks but cannot reshape the core therapeutic work.

title: Registered nurses
rationale: Nursing involves substantial physical presence, hands-on patient care, emotional support, and real-time clinical judgment that AI cannot replicate, though AI assists with documentation, monitoring, and some diagnostic support. Healthcare demand is highly elastic - an aging population, chronic disease management, and preference for home/community care create enormous latent demand that cheaper/faster healthcare delivery would unlock. However, the disruption score of 5 places this in the resilient category, as the core work remains fundamentally human-centered despite AI augmentation of peripheral tasks.

title: Roofers
rationale: Roofing is almost entirely physical work requiring manual dexterity, balance, and presence at unpredictable outdoor job sites - AI has minimal relevance to core tasks like installing shingles, measuring materials, or working at heights. Demand for roofing is relatively inelastic as it's driven by necessary building maintenance and construction rather than discretionary spending, with limited ability to expand significantly even if costs dropped. The occupation falls clearly into the resilient category due to very low disruption from AI technologies.

title: School and career counselors and advisors
rationale: AI significantly disrupts the knowledge-work portions of counseling through tools that can analyze student data, match interests to careers, provide initial assessments, and offer 24/7 guidance, but the core human elements of empathy, relationship-building, and complex emotional support remain essential. Demand elasticity is high because AI-powered counseling tools could dramatically expand access to personalized guidance - currently many students receive limited counseling due to high counselor-to-student ratios, and cheaper AI-assisted counseling could serve underserved populations and provide more frequent, personalized support. The combination of moderate-to-high disruption with high elasticity suggests counselors will increasingly work alongside AI tools to serve many more students effectively.

title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
rationale: This occupation faces high disruption as core tasks like market analysis, trade execution, portfolio recommendations, and client research are increasingly automated through AI-powered trading systems, robo-advisors, and algorithmic analysis tools. The BLS already notes that automated trading systems have reduced demand for securities traders and simpler transactions can be completed online without brokers. However, demand elasticity is low because financial services consumption is relatively fixed - individuals and institutions don't dramatically increase their trading or investment advisory needs just because costs drop, and regulatory constraints limit market expansion. The combination of high AI disruption with constrained demand growth points to significant headcount contraction in this field.

title: Skincare specialists
rationale: Skincare specialists' core work involves hands-on physical treatments, skin evaluation through touch and visual inspection, and direct client interaction that requires human presence and judgment. While AI might assist with skin analysis apps or product recommendations, the fundamental work of performing facials, treatments, and building client relationships remains inherently human-centered. Demand shows moderate elasticity as lower costs could make skincare services accessible to more people and increase frequency of visits, but the work's physical nature and moderate disruption level place it in the resilient category.

title: Small engine mechanics
rationale: Small engine mechanics work is predominantly hands-on physical repair requiring direct manipulation of mechanical components, diagnosis through tactile feedback, and real-world problem-solving in varied environments - areas where AI has minimal impact. While some diagnostic equipment may incorporate AI features, the core work of disassembling engines, replacing parts, and mechanical troubleshooting remains fundamentally manual. Demand elasticity is low because equipment repair needs are driven by usage and wear patterns rather than cost - people don't get more repairs just because they're cheaper, and regulatory/safety requirements limit how much diagnostic work can be automated. The physical, tactile nature of mechanical repair makes this occupation highly resilient to AI disruption.

title: Social workers
rationale: Social work involves substantial human-centered activities requiring empathy, relationship-building, crisis intervention, and complex situational assessment that AI cannot replicate, though AI could assist with documentation, resource matching, and case management tasks. Demand for social work services is highly elastic - there is massive unmet need for mental health support, family services, and community assistance that is currently constrained by cost and availability. However, the disruption score of 4 places this occupation in the resilient category, as the core interpersonal and therapeutic aspects of social work remain fundamentally human-dependent despite potential AI augmentation of administrative functions.

title: Special effects artists and animators
rationale: AI is highly disruptive to special effects and animation work, as generative AI tools can now create sophisticated 2D/3D models, animations, and visual effects that previously required extensive manual work. However, demand elasticity is high because cheaper, faster animation production unlocks massive latent demand across gaming, streaming content, social media, advertising, and emerging platforms like VR/AR. The cost barrier that previously limited animation to big-budget productions is rapidly falling, enabling more creators and businesses to incorporate high-quality visual content.

title: Stationary engineers and boiler operators
rationale: The disruption score is moderate because while AI can assist with monitoring systems through advanced sensors and predictive maintenance analytics, the core work remains highly physical - operating valves, inspecting equipment, performing repairs, and responding to mechanical failures in real-world industrial environments. The elasticity score is low because demand for boiler and mechanical system operation is tied to fixed building infrastructure and industrial capacity - making systems 10x more efficient doesn't create demand for 10x more buildings or factories. Since disruption is moderate (4) rather than high (≥7), this occupation falls into the resilient category where AI touches peripheral monitoring tasks but doesn't reshape the fundamentally hands-on nature of the work.

title: Surgical assistants and technologists
rationale: Surgical assistants and technologists perform primarily hands-on, physical tasks in sterile operating room environments that require real-time human presence, dexterity, and immediate response to surgeons' needs - AI cannot replace the physical manipulation of instruments, patient positioning, or sterile field maintenance. While AI may assist with some peripheral tasks like inventory management or scheduling, the core work remains fundamentally manual and requires human judgment in unpredictable surgical situations. Demand for surgical procedures is relatively inelastic as it's driven by medical necessity rather than cost considerations - even if AI made certain aspects more efficient, the number of surgeries needed is primarily determined by patient health conditions, not productivity gains.

title: Surveyors
rationale: Surveying involves substantial fieldwork requiring physical presence to take precise measurements in varied terrain and weather conditions, though modern surveyors do use GPS, GIS, and CAD technologies for data collection and processing. The disruption score reflects that while AI can assist with data analysis, mapping, and report generation, the core work of establishing legal property boundaries requires on-site measurement and verification that cannot be fully automated. Demand elasticity is low because surveying is primarily driven by regulatory requirements for property transactions, construction permits, and legal documentation - making surveys cheaper wouldn't significantly increase the number of property sales or construction projects. The net effect is resilient because the physical measurement requirements and regulatory nature of the work limit AI's ability to reshape the core occupation.

title: Tax examiners and collectors, and revenue agents
rationale: Tax examination and collection work is predominantly digital and analytical, involving pattern recognition, document review, data analysis, and rule-based decision making - all areas where AI excels and is already being deployed. Modern AI can process tax returns, identify discrepancies, flag suspicious deductions, and even conduct initial assessments far more efficiently than humans. However, demand for tax enforcement is relatively fixed - governments need a certain level of compliance monitoring regardless of cost, and taxpayers don't voluntarily increase their audit frequency when it becomes cheaper. The productivity gains from AI will primarily translate to fewer workers needed rather than expanded tax enforcement activities.

title: Teacher assistants
rationale: Teacher assistants face moderate-to-high disruption as AI can increasingly handle administrative tasks like recordkeeping, grade calculations, and basic instructional reinforcement through personalized tutoring systems. However, the core human elements—supervising students, providing emotional support, managing behavior, and working with special needs students—remain essential. Demand elasticity is high because personalized educational support is currently constrained by cost and staffing limitations; AI-enhanced teacher assistants could dramatically expand access to individualized attention, especially in underserved schools and for special education needs. The combination of meaningful AI augmentation with strong latent demand for personalized educational support suggests the occupation will evolve rather than contract.

title: Technical writers
rationale: Technical writing is highly susceptible to AI disruption as it involves core tasks like writing, editing, content creation, and information synthesis that AI excels at - current tools can already draft technical documentation, create user guides, and convert complex information into accessible formats. Demand elasticity is moderate because while cheaper technical writing could unlock some latent demand (more products getting proper documentation, smaller companies creating user guides), growth is constrained by the fact that documentation needs are typically tied to specific products and regulatory requirements rather than being infinitely expandable. The net effect is restructure because technical writers will need to shift toward higher-level skills like user experience research, cross-functional collaboration, and strategic content planning while AI handles routine documentation tasks.

title: Telecommunications technicians
rationale: Telecommunications technicians perform predominantly physical, hands-on work including climbing towers, installing cables, splicing fiber optics, and troubleshooting hardware in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI might assist with some diagnostic tasks or record-keeping, the core work of physical installation, repair, and maintenance of telecommunications infrastructure requires human presence and manual dexterity. Demand for telecommunications services is relatively inelastic - people and businesses need a fixed amount of connectivity regardless of installation costs, and productivity gains in installation speed don't translate to dramatically more infrastructure being built. The occupation remains resilient as AI cannot meaningfully change the fundamentally physical nature of the work.

title: Training and development managers
rationale: Training and development managers face high disruption as AI can automate content creation, personalize learning paths, assess training needs through data analysis, and generate training materials - core knowledge work activities that comprise much of their role. However, demand elasticity is moderate because while cheaper AI-powered training tools will expand access to professional development, organizational training budgets and regulatory requirements for certain types of training create structural limits on demand expansion. The combination of high disruption and moderate elasticity places this occupation in the restructure category, where managers who adapt to leverage AI tools for content creation and data-driven insights will thrive, while those who don't will be displaced.

title: Training and development specialists
rationale: Training and development work is highly disrupted by AI, as core tasks like creating training materials, designing curricula, assessing needs, and delivering content are predominantly knowledge-based and digital. AI tools already assist with content creation, personalized learning paths, and automated assessment. Demand elasticity is high because cheaper, faster training development could unlock massive latent demand - organizations currently constrained by cost and time could provide more frequent upskilling, personalized learning, and compliance training. As technology accelerates and skills become obsolete faster, the need for continuous workforce development will expand dramatically when AI makes it more accessible and affordable.

title: Transportation, storage, and distribution managers
rationale: Transportation, storage, and distribution managers face moderate disruption as AI tools can assist with route optimization, inventory tracking, demand forecasting, and data analysis, but the core work remains heavily focused on human leadership, staff management, client relationships, and real-world operational oversight that requires physical presence and interpersonal skills. Demand elasticity is low because logistics needs are largely tied to existing economic activity - making supply chains 10x more efficient doesn't create 10x more goods to move, and regulatory constraints limit how much operational efficiency translates to expanded demand. The disruption score of 6 places this in the resilient category, where AI enhances capabilities but doesn't fundamentally reshape the occupation's core human-centered management responsibilities.

title: Tutors
rationale: Tutoring faces high disruption as AI can already provide personalized instruction, generate practice problems, explain concepts, and track student progress - core tutoring functions that are predominantly cognitive and language-based. However, demand elasticity is very high because personalized education has been historically limited by cost and access, with vast latent demand from students who cannot afford private tutoring. AI-powered tutoring tools can dramatically reduce costs and increase availability, potentially expanding the market from hundreds of thousands to millions of students globally. While AI will reshape how tutoring is delivered, the massive expansion in accessible personalized education will likely create more opportunities for human tutors who can provide emotional support, motivation, and complex problem-solving that complements AI tools.

title: Urban and regional planners
rationale: Urban and regional planners work primarily with digital tools (GIS software, data analysis, visualization tools) and knowledge-based tasks like analyzing data, creating reports, and developing plans - areas where AI is already making significant inroads. AI can increasingly handle data analysis, pattern recognition in urban development, and even generate planning recommendations based on multiple variables. However, demand elasticity is low because planning needs are largely driven by population growth and regulatory requirements rather than cost - communities don't suddenly need 10x more planning when it becomes cheaper, and government budgets constrain expansion regardless of efficiency gains.

title: Veterinarians
rationale: Veterinary work involves substantial hands-on physical examination, surgery, and direct animal interaction that AI cannot replicate, though AI can assist with diagnostics, imaging analysis, and record-keeping. The disruption score of 5 reflects this mixed nature - significant knowledge work components but core tasks requiring physical presence and manual dexterity. Elasticity is high (7) because cheaper diagnostic tools and AI-assisted care could expand access to veterinary services, particularly in underserved areas, and enable more preventive care as costs decrease. However, since disruption remains moderate at 5, this falls into the resilient category where AI enhances rather than reshapes the core occupation.

title: Veterinary technologists and technicians
rationale: Veterinary technologists and technicians perform a mix of hands-on animal care (restraining, bathing, administering medications) and technical tasks (x-rays, lab tests, record keeping). While AI could assist with diagnostic analysis, record keeping, and some laboratory work, the core job requires physical interaction with animals, manual dexterity for procedures, and real-time assessment of animal behavior and condition. The demand elasticity is moderate - cheaper veterinary services could expand access to pet care and enable more preventive treatments, but growth is constrained by the number of pets and regulatory requirements. Since disruption is moderate (5) and doesn't reach the high threshold (7+), this occupation falls into the resilient category despite moderate elasticity.

title: Waiters and waitresses
rationale: Disruption is moderate because while AI can handle some peripheral tasks like order-taking through kiosks and payment processing, the core work remains fundamentally physical and interpersonal - carrying food, providing personalized customer service, handling complex requests, and managing the social dynamics of dining. Elasticity is low because demand for restaurant dining is relatively fixed by factors like meal frequency, restaurant capacity, and consumer budgets; making service faster or cheaper doesn't meaningfully increase how often people dine out. The net effect is resilient because AI touches mainly the ordering/payment periphery while the essential human elements of hospitality, physical service, and real-time problem-solving remain central to the role.

title: Water transportation workers
rationale: Water transportation work is fundamentally physical and requires real-world presence on vessels, with core tasks like operating ships, maintaining equipment, and ensuring safety being largely manual and hands-on. While AI might assist with some navigation systems or logistics planning, the essential work of physically operating and maintaining vessels at sea cannot be digitized. Demand for water transportation is relatively inelastic as it's driven by fixed shipping needs for cargo and passengers - making ships 10x more efficient wouldn't create 10x more demand for shipping services, as global trade volumes and passenger travel are constrained by broader economic factors rather than transportation costs alone.

title: Wind turbine technicians
rationale: Wind turbine technicians perform predominantly physical work requiring climbing towers, manual repairs, and hands-on maintenance in challenging outdoor environments that AI cannot replicate. While AI might assist with diagnostic monitoring and data analysis, the core work of physically inspecting, climbing, repairing, and maintaining turbines at heights remains fundamentally human-dependent. The elasticity is high due to rapid expansion of wind energy infrastructure driven by climate goals and energy transition needs - cheaper maintenance could accelerate wind farm deployment. However, with disruption remaining low-to-moderate, this occupation falls into the resilient category where AI enhances peripheral tasks but doesn't reshape the core physical work.

title: Woodworkers
rationale: Woodworking remains predominantly physical and hands-on work requiring manual dexterity, physical strength, and real-world problem-solving with materials that vary in grain, density, and characteristics. While CNC machines and computer-controlled equipment assist with precision cutting and shaping, the core tasks still involve physical manipulation of heavy materials, hand-tool work, assembly, finishing, and quality assessment that requires human judgment and tactile feedback. Demand elasticity is constrained by the fact that furniture and cabinet needs are relatively fixed - people don't suddenly buy 10x more cabinets if they become cheaper, and much of the market is driven by replacement cycles and home construction/renovation patterns rather than pure cost sensitivity. The low disruption score keeps this occupation in the resilient category despite moderate demand constraints.

title: Community health workers
rationale: Community health workers primarily engage in relationship-building, cultural mediation, and in-person advocacy work that requires deep community ties and human presence. While AI could assist with some administrative tasks like data collection and resource matching, the core work of building trust, conducting home visits, and providing culturally sensitive support remains fundamentally human-centered. Demand elasticity is high because cheaper delivery of community health services could dramatically expand access in underserved areas where cost barriers currently limit coverage. However, the moderate disruption score keeps this occupation in the resilient category, as AI touches only peripheral aspects of the work.

title: Compensation, benefits, and job analysis specialists
rationale: This occupation involves predominantly analytical and data-driven tasks that AI can increasingly handle - compensation analysis, benefits research, job classification, and report generation are all within AI's capabilities. Current AI tools can already perform complex data analysis, benchmarking, and even draft job descriptions and compensation recommendations. However, demand elasticity is low because organizations have relatively fixed needs for compensation structure and benefits administration regardless of cost - they don't suddenly need 10x more job classifications or compensation analyses just because AI makes it cheaper and faster.

title: Computer and information research scientists
rationale: Computer and information research scientists work almost entirely in digital domains - developing algorithms, programming languages, software systems, and conducting computational experiments - making them highly susceptible to AI disruption as these are core AI capabilities. However, demand elasticity is high because cheaper and faster research capabilities will unlock massive latent demand for new technologies, AI applications, cybersecurity solutions, and data analysis across all industries. The 20% projected job growth despite high AI disruption suggests the field will expand rather than contract as productivity gains enable more ambitious research projects and faster innovation cycles.

title: Concierges
rationale: Concierge work involves significant information processing, reservation making, and communication tasks that AI can increasingly handle through chatbots, automated booking systems, and digital assistants, earning a disruption score of 6. However, the core human elements of greeting guests, building relationships, and handling complex personal requests still require physical presence and emotional intelligence. Demand elasticity is low (3) because concierge services are typically bundled into existing facilities rather than purchased separately - hotels, apartments, and offices won't dramatically expand concierge headcount even if AI makes individual concierges more productive. The combination of moderate-high disruption with low elasticity suggests restructuring, where successful concierges will need to adapt by focusing more on high-touch personal service while AI handles routine information and booking tasks.

title: Construction and building inspectors
rationale: Construction and building inspectors face moderate disruption as AI can assist with report writing, code compliance checking, and data analysis, but the core work requires physical presence at construction sites, hands-on equipment use, and real-world judgment about structural safety. Demand elasticity is low because inspection requirements are largely fixed by regulations and safety standards - making inspections faster or cheaper doesn't create demand for more inspections of the same buildings. The physical, regulatory, and liability-driven nature of this work keeps it in the resilient category despite some digital augmentation potential.

title: Construction equipment operators
rationale: Construction equipment operation is fundamentally physical work requiring real-time coordination with heavy machinery in unpredictable outdoor environments, making it largely resistant to AI disruption. While some modern equipment incorporates GPS and computerized controls that AI could enhance, the core tasks of maneuvering bulldozers, excavators, and other heavy machinery require human presence, spatial awareness, and physical dexterity. Demand for construction equipment operators is relatively inelastic as infrastructure projects have fixed scope requirements - making roads 10x faster doesn't create 10x more road projects. The occupation remains resilient due to low disruption potential despite inelastic demand.

title: Construction laborers and helpers
rationale: Construction laborers and helpers perform predominantly physical tasks like moving materials, digging, cleaning sites, and operating hand tools - work that requires real-world presence and manual dexterity that AI cannot replicate. While some advanced equipment may incorporate digital controls, the core work remains hands-on and physical. Demand elasticity is low because construction projects have fixed labor requirements regardless of productivity gains - you still need the same number of people to move materials and assist tradespeople on each jobsite. The physical, location-specific nature of this work makes it highly resilient to AI disruption.

title: Cooks
rationale: Cooking involves predominantly physical, hands-on tasks requiring real-time sensory judgment, manual dexterity, and unpredictable problem-solving that AI cannot replicate. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like inventory management or recipe suggestions, the core work of food preparation, cooking techniques, taste adjustment, and kitchen coordination remains fundamentally human. Demand elasticity is moderate because cheaper food preparation could expand dining out and food delivery markets, but growth is constrained by physical kitchen capacity, food safety regulations, and consumer dining patterns. The low disruption score places this firmly in the resilient category despite moderate demand elasticity.

title: Correctional officers and bailiffs
rationale: This occupation involves predominantly physical presence, human judgment in unpredictable situations, and direct supervision of people - core tasks that AI cannot meaningfully assist with. While some administrative tasks like report writing might benefit from AI tools, the essential work of maintaining security, conducting searches, and managing human interactions remains fundamentally unchanged. Demand for correctional services is relatively fixed by crime rates, court decisions, and incarceration policies rather than cost considerations, so productivity gains would not expand the need for these services. The low disruption score keeps this firmly in the resilient category despite very low demand elasticity.

title: Craft and fine artists
rationale: AI significantly disrupts many core artistic tasks - digital artists already use AI tools extensively, illustrators can leverage AI for concept generation and refinement, and even traditional artists increasingly use AI for portfolio development and marketing. However, the creative conceptualization, personal artistic vision, and physical craftsmanship remain distinctly human. Demand elasticity is high because cheaper and faster art production unlocks massive latent demand - businesses need more visual content, personalized art becomes accessible to broader markets, and the explosion of digital platforms creates endless opportunities for visual content. While AI transforms how art is made, the democratization of art creation and the insatiable appetite for visual content means total demand for artistic output will likely expand faster than productivity gains reduce the need for human artists.

title: Credit counselors
rationale: Credit counseling involves substantial knowledge work - analyzing financial data, creating debt management plans, explaining complex financial concepts, and negotiating with creditors - tasks that AI can increasingly assist with or automate. AI tools can already help with financial analysis, document preparation, and basic client communications. However, the work still requires significant human judgment for complex situations and interpersonal skills for building client rapport. Demand elasticity is moderate because while cheaper credit counseling could expand access (especially for underserved populations), growth is constrained by regulatory requirements, the personal nature of financial distress, and the fact that people generally seek these services only when in financial trouble rather than proactively.

title: Dancers and choreographers
rationale: Dance and choreography are fundamentally physical, embodied art forms requiring human presence, athleticism, and real-time interaction with audiences and other performers. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like music selection or administrative duties, the core work of physical movement, creative expression through the body, and live performance remains entirely human-dependent. Demand shows moderate elasticity as social media and digital platforms create new venues for dance content, and cheaper production tools could expand opportunities for dance education and performance. However, the low disruption score dominates, placing this occupation in the resilient category where AI touches only the periphery of the work.

title: Dental and ophthalmic laboratory technicians and medical appliance technicians
rationale: AI and automation are already disrupting core tasks through 3D printing, automated lens manufacturing, and design software that can generate dental appliances from digital impressions. However, the work still requires significant manual craftsmanship, precision fitting, and quality control that involves physical manipulation of materials. Demand elasticity is low because the need for dental appliances, eyewear, and medical devices is largely driven by medical necessity rather than cost - people don't get more prosthetics or dentures just because they're cheaper to produce. The BLS projects declining employment due to labor-saving technologies, indicating this is a restructure scenario where workers must adapt to new digital tools and automated processes.

title: Dental hygienists
rationale: Dental hygienists perform predominantly hands-on, physical work requiring direct patient contact, manual dexterity with specialized tools, and real-time assessment of oral health conditions that AI cannot replicate.
